summary: All tests pass. The fix was in `middleware.go:18` — the middleware was checking the raw `r.URL.Path` while the router was normalizing it first via `path.Clean()`. An attacker sending `//api/admin` would bypass the `strings.HasPrefix(path, "/api/")` check (since `//api/admin` doesn't start with `/api/`), but the router would clean it to `/api/admin` and serve the protected handler. The fix applies the same `path.Clean()` normalization in the middleware before doing the prefix check.
